attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put the clock on the desk
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

The scene depicts an office or study area with wooden furniture against a light-colored wall. The primary objects relevant to the task are a desk, a cabinet, a clock, and a lamp.

1. Objects and their types:
   - The desk is a large, flat wooden surface with a visible wood grain pattern. It is positioned in the foreground and appears to be supported by legs (one leg is visible in the second image). It functions as a receptacle for holding objects.
   - The cabinet is a wooden unit with two doors, mounted above the desk. It is also made of wood with a similar grain pattern. It is a receptacle and is currently closed.
   - The clock is a square, grey-cased analog clock with a white face and black numerals. It is currently placed on the desk, in front of the cabinet. In the domain, it is classified as an object.
   - The lamp is positioned on top of the cabinet. It has a cylindrical silver base and a black lampshade, with a warm yellow glow emanating from within. It is an object and is currently turned on (isOn is true).
   - A black rectangular object, possibly a smartphone or a small tablet, is also on the desk, to the right of the clock.
   - A CD or DVD disc is visible on the desk surface in the second image, near the back edge.

2. Quantities:
   - 1 desk (receptacle)
   - 1 cabinet (receptacle)
   - 1 clock (object)
   - 1 lamp (object)
   - 1 black rectangular object (object)
   - 1 disc (object)

3. Spatial relationships:
   - The clock is on the desk (atLocation ?a clock would be true if the agent is at the desk).
   - The lamp is on top of the cabinet.
   - The cabinet is mounted above the desk.
   - The black rectangular object and the disc are also on the desk.
   - The agent is not currently at any object's location (initial state).

4. Current state of objects relevant to the planning task:
   - The clock is not being held by the agent (holdsAny ?a is false for the agent).
   - The clock is on the desk, not in any receptacle (inReceptacle ?o ?r is false for the clock).
   - The lamp is on (isOn lamp is true).
   - The cabinet is closed (opened cabinet is false).
   - No objects are being held by the agent.

5. Visual properties:
   - The desk and cabinet are made of medium-brown wood with a visible grain.
   - The clock has a grey casing, a white face, and black hands and numerals.
   - The lamp has a metallic silver base and a black fabric shade, emitting a warm yellow light.
   - The black rectangular object is sleek and dark, contrasting with the wooden desk.
   - The disc is reflective with a rainbow sheen, typical of optical media.

The instruction "Put the clock on the desk" is already satisfied in the current scene, as the clock is already on the desk. No action is required to move the clock, but the agent may need to interact with it or confirm its position.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:

attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object

    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)

    :effect (and

 )

;; agent cools some object

            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )

    :precondition
            (and

            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )

attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object

    :precondition (and

            )
    :effect (and

            )
 )

;; agent cools some object

    :precondition (and

            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject

    :precondition (and

            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)

(define (problem put_clock_on_desk)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        desk - receptacle
        cabinet - receptacle
        clock - object
        smartphone - object
        lamp - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable cabinet)
        (not (opened cabinet))
        (inReceptacle clock desk)
        (inReceptacle smartphone desk)
        (isOn lamp)
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 clock))
        (not (atLocation agent1 smartphone))
        (not (atLocation agent1 lamp))
        (not (atLocation agent1 desk))
        (not (atLocation agent1 cabinet))

        (not (isHot clock))
        (not (isCool clock))
        (not (isSliced clock))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle clock desk)
        (atLocation agent1 clock)
    ))
)

attempts.problem:
(define (problem put_clock_on_desk)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        desk - receptacle
        cabinet - receptacle
        clock - object
        smartphone - object
        lamp - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable cabinet)
        (not (opened cabinet))
        (inReceptacle clock desk)
        (inReceptacle smartphone desk)
        (isOn lamp)
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 clock))
        (not (atLocation agent1 smartphone))
        (not (atLocation agent1 lamp))
        (not (atLocation agent1 desk))
        (not (atLocation agent1 cabinet))
        (not (isClean clock))
        (not (isHot clock))
        (not (isCool clock))
        (not (isSliced clock))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle clock desk)
        (atLocation agent1 clock)
    ))
)

final.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put the clock on the desk
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene contains a wooden desk with a matching wooden cabinet positioned above it. On the desk, there is a square, grey analog clock with a white face and black numerals, and a black smartphone. The clock is located on the left side of the desk, and the smartphone is to its right. A black office chair is positioned in front of the desk, partially visible from below. On the cabinet above the desk, there is a black lamp with a cylindrical base and a lit yellow lampshade, and a black rectangular object, possibly a monitor or a picture frame, is placed next to the lamp.

In terms of domain-relevant objects:
- The desk and the cabinet are receptacles, as they can hold objects.
- The clock and the smartphone are objects (not receptacles).
- The lamp is an object that can be toggled (isOn predicate applies).
- The chair is not relevant to the domain.

Current state:
- The clock is on the desk (atLocation agent clock is not true, as the agent is not yet at the clock).
- The smartphone is on the desk.
- The lamp is on (isOn is true).
- The cabinet is closed (opened is false, and it is openable).
- No object is being held by the agent (holdsAny is false).
- The clock is not clean, hot, cool, or sliced.
- The agent is not at any location yet.

Spatial relationships:
- The clock is on the desk surface.
- The smartphone is on the desk surface, to the right of the clock.
- The lamp and the black rectangular object are on top of the cabinet.
- The cabinet is mounted on the wall above the desk.
- The chair is in front of the desk.

Colors and distinguishing features:
- The clock has a grey casing, white face, and black hands and numerals.
- The smartphone is black and rectangular.
- The lamp has a black shade and a metallic silver base, with a glowing yellow light.
- The desk and cabinet are made of brown wood with visible grain.
- The chair is black.

The instruction “Put the clock on the desk” is already satisfied, as the clock is already on the desk. However, if the task implies moving the clock from one location to another on the desk, or if the agent must interact with it, the agent must first go to the clock’s location.

(define (problem put_clock_on_desk)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        desk - receptacle
        cabinet - receptacle

        smartphone - object
        lamp - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable cabinet)
        (not (opened cabinet))
        (inReceptacle clock desk)
        (inReceptacle smartphone desk)
        (isOn lamp)
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 clock))
        (not (atLocation agent1 smartphone))
        (not (atLocation agent1 lamp))
        (not (atLocation agent1 desk))
        (not (atLocation agent1 cabinet))
        (not (isClean clock))
        (not (isHot clock))
        (not (isCool clock))
        (not (isSliced clock))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle clock desk)
        (atLocation agent1 clock)
    ))
)

final.problem:
(define (problem put_clock_on_desk)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        desk - receptacle
        cabinet - receptacle
        clock - object
        smartphone - object
        lamp - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable cabinet)
        (not (opened cabinet))
        (inReceptacle clock desk)
        (inReceptacle smartphone desk)
        (isOn lamp)
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 clock))
        (not (atLocation agent1 smartphone))
        (not (atLocation agent1 lamp))
        (not (atLocation agent1 desk))
        (not (atLocation agent1 cabinet))
        (not (isClean clock))
        (not (isHot clock))
        (not (isCool clock))
        (not (isSliced clock))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle clock desk)
        (atLocation agent1 clock)
    ))
)
